sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 4-year-old mother, presented with a knee injury sustained while skiing in Vermont last weekend.
She fell and her knee "popped" and has been hurting ever since.
She was unable to bear weight and had to be taken down by the ski patrol.
The pain is localized to the outside of the knee and feels like it will buckle when bearing weight.
She has difficulty bending and straightening the knee, but can do so with some pain.
There is no numbness or tingling in the toes, and she can feel them.
She is otherwise generally healthy and has no high blood pressure, diabetes, or other significant medical conditions.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600-800 mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has edema and an effusion over the right knee.
There is pain to palpation of the right lateral knee.
There is decreased flexion and extension of the right knee.
The Lachman sign is positive.
The dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse are palpable.
There is no leg edema in the ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a suspected ACL (anterior cruciate ligament) rupture, likely sustained during a skiing accident.
Problem list: ACL rupture; suspected; unknown; knee pain; unknown.
Plan:
I ordered a knee MRI to confirm the diagnosis.
I will continue the patient on ibuprofen 600-800 mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
I will also provide a knee brace to support the knee.
I will review the MRI results with the patient in the next few days to discuss further management.
Follow-up: I will see the patient again soon to review the MRI results and discuss the next steps in management.
